tasks.description: Generate a behavior manifest beside the existing registry artifacts so component behavior metadata comes from the real design system pipeline, while keeping all work critically on `geoquant/streaming-ui`.
steps: Codegen emits a new generated behavior artifact, e.g. `packages/kumo/ai/component-behavior.json`, from the same workflow that updates `component-registry.json` and `schemas.ts`.
Generated behavior metadata includes at least generative support status, layout role, bindable props, emitted events, wrapper kind, and validation hints for representative components.
Behavior-only fields that cannot be inferred are provided by a small explicit overlay instead of new scattered manual allowlists.
A newly added component such as `TimeseriesChart` appears in generated output with explicit runtime/generative capability state.
Task notes and resulting artifacts preserve the constraint that implementation must stay on branch `geoquant/streaming-ui`.

tasks.description: Add a `UITree` to `AppSpec` compatibility adapter so `AppSpec` can become the canonical runtime model without breaking playground behavior, and keep the rollout critically scoped to `geoquant/streaming-ui`.
steps: A typed adapter exists that converts legacy `UITree` input into valid `AppSpec` output.
Legacy `visible` and `action` semantics map into `AppSpec` visibility and event semantics without requiring per-component manual runtime rules outside the generated behavior manifest plus overlay.
A reverse or renderable projection path is defined clearly enough that `UITree` callers remain supported during migration.
Compatibility tests cover shared cases for actions, visibility, children, and minimal stateless trees.
The adapter rollout keeps implementation constrained to `geoquant/streaming-ui`.

tasks.description: Converge renderer and runtime execution onto an AppSpec-backed internal path while keeping the playground's effective behavior stable and all work critically on `geoquant/streaming-ui`.
steps: An AppSpec-first renderer/runtime path exists and can execute the same shared cases that the current UITree path handles.
`UITreeRenderer` or equivalent compatibility entry delegates through the canonical runtime path instead of maintaining a separate peer execution model.
Behavior classification used by rendering comes from generated behavior metadata rather than scattered hard-coded sets wherever practical.
Focused playground compatibility tests still pass for existing prompt/tool flows after the renderer convergence work.
The convergence work remains on branch `geoquant/streaming-ui`.

tasks.description: Make the generated catalog always fresh for component changes so new library capabilities become visible to prompts, validation, and runtime support quickly, with rollout critically on `geoquant/streaming-ui`.
steps: Changing or adding a component under `packages/kumo/src/components/**` updates generated catalog artifacts through the in-repo codegen workflow.
The same codegen workflow regenerates registry, schemas, behavior manifest, and prompt-facing manifest together.
CI or drift validation fails when component or relevant demo changes are made without regenerated artifacts.
New components are not silently absent from prompt/runtime visibility; they are either included or explicitly marked excluded with a reason.
The freshness pipeline and validation remain scoped to `geoquant/streaming-ui`.

tasks.description: Simplify streaming and loadable APIs around one internal runtime while preserving current host-first playground behavior and keeping the work critically on `geoquant/streaming-ui`.
steps: The strict JSONL patch parser and repair behavior still function for existing streaming flows.
`loadable` retains host-first, DOM-first ergonomics while using the canonical runtime internally.
Legacy callers can still send `UITree` or equivalent patch streams during the migration phase.
A clearer single-runtime internal story is documented in code interfaces or module boundaries for streaming/loadable paths.
The streaming convergence work remains on `geoquant/streaming-ui`.

tasks.description: Document the competitive narrative and migration story so Kumo is clearly positioned as a catalog-derived generative/runtime system rather than a handwritten DSL competitor, with docs changes critically staying on `geoquant/streaming-ui`.
steps: Docs describe Kumo's advantage as generated catalog + behavior metadata coming from the real design system surface.
Docs explain that prompts, validation, wrappers, renderers, and streaming consume the same generated truth.
Docs explain the migration stance: `AppSpec` is canonical internally while `UITree` remains a compatibility input during rollout.
The narrative explicitly differentiates Kumo from handwritten schema competitors without depending on or adopting `json-render`.
Documentation work remains on branch `geoquant/streaming-ui`.

patterns: All implementation is critically constrained to branch geoquant/streaming-ui.
Registry codegen already emits `component-registry.json`, `schemas.ts`, and `component-manifest.ts` from real component source and docs demos.
Playground behavior must not break during rollout even if underlying APIs change.
Kumo's differentiator is autogenerated catalog fidelity from the real design system, not a second handwritten DSL.
nonGoals: Breaking current playground behavior during migration
Immediate removal of UITree as an accepted input format
Multi-platform runtime expansion beyond Kumo's web-native focus
Rewriting every renderer or streaming primitive before compatibility adapters exist
